title: eriogonum abertianum subsp. pinetorum
intro: Eriogonum abertianum subsp. pinetorum is a subspecies of the desert buckwheat plant, a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum abertianum subsp. pinetorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
